questions.question: Explain the concepts of function overloading and recursion in C++. Provide an example of each and discuss how they are resolved at compile time.
keyPoints: Function overloading: multiple functions with the same name, different parameter list, resolved by signature.
Recursion: a function calls itself directly or indirectly, requiring a base case to terminate.
Example: overload add(int, int) and add(double, double); recursion: factorial function.
The compiler uses name mangling and parameter types to differentiate overloaded functions.

questions.question: Describe pass-by-reference in C++ and explain the use of const and static qualifiers in function parameters. Provide a code snippet illustrating these concepts.
keyPoints: Pass‑by‑reference allows a function to modify the original variable using a reference (&) or pointer.
A const reference guarantees that the function will not modify the argument.
A static parameter retains its value between calls and is shared across invocations of the function.
Example: void foo(const int& x, int& y, static int z);

questions.question: Discuss the C++ type casting operators: static_cast, dynamic_cast, const_cast, and reinterpret_cast. Provide scenarios where each is appropriate, including examples involving class hierarchies and safe downcasting.
keyPoints: static_cast: compile‑time conversions, e.g., numeric type conversions and base‑to‑derived conversions (with caution).
dynamic_cast: safe downcasting in a polymorphic hierarchy; returns nullptr for pointers on failure.
const_cast: adds or removes const/volatile qualifiers; only used when the underlying object is not actually const.
reinterpret_cast: low‑level bit‑pattern reinterpreting; unsafe and implementation‑defined.

questions.question: Explain lambda expressions and function pointers in C++. Show how lambda capture lists work, how they can be converted to function pointers, and give an example where a lambda is used as a callback to a higher‑order function.
keyPoints: Lambda: an anonymous function object with a capture list [], optional parameters (), and a body {}.
Capture list can capture by value [=], by reference [&], or specific variables.
A stateless lambda (no capture) can be converted to a function pointer with the matching signature.
Example: std::vector<int> v; std::sort(v.begin(), v.end(), [](int a, int b){ return a > b; });
